channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: FIRE is mental illness — 260 replies — 149.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
FIRE is mental illness

Open thread • Replies: 260 • Updated: 2026-05-26 12:47 UTC

OP

No woman, no kids, $25k YEARLY spend.
You're not supposed to check out of society in your 30s and spend the rest of your life relaxing. What is wrong with modern men?

Early replies (first 3)

62259938
>>62259935
Keep slaving away goyman

62259945
>>62259935
Nice bait thread faggot

62259968
>>62259935
You can't really afford to marry on FIRE because one divorce ruins a decade+ of saving and then you have to work again. If a woman finds out you're FIRE she will absolutely divorce rape you too since the wealth disparity between you and her is so great she'll walk away with a ludicrous amount of money.

Huge downside of FIRE, you can never marry.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62265896
>>62264378
>This is not the person I would mock, he actually did fucking make it. I have no idea how you get 12 years in work make near 2 million and also a fully paid house jesus christ wow.
the stemfag market was goated 12 years ago, now you have to be the throte goat to find anything

62265903
>>62265888
My gf keeps eating the cum before it reaches her pussy though.

62265928
>>62265903
Wrong hole.
Blowjobs are nice but shoot for a simultaneous orgasm. By far the best sensation the human body can experience and it makes women more loyal to you (if they can feel such a thing).

62268369
>>62263653
>>62264007
Eh, it's still hot though. You get bored after 10 years of fucking 1 woman.

62268422
>>62259968
>FIRE
>implying it's the end
There's no reason you can't still invest. In fact, you should considering how many unexpected expenses can pop up unless you're filthy rich once you're 'done' with FIRE.

62268475
>>62259968
>Huge downside of FIRE, you can never marry.
>never marry
>downside

62268693
>>62259935
It's kind of odd to me that people with such a net worth only ever consider retiring to do nothing.
A more natural progression seems to be managing your own wealth. When your net worth is so high you can only grow it by 5-10% per year, improving your portfolio's performance by a few % (not only from growth but also downside protection/risk management) has a huge impact.
Many probably buy into the "nothing beats buying and holding the index forever" narrative, and many don't want to bother dealing with finance things, but at some wealth:salary ratio it has to become unreasonable to avoid it.

62268709
>>62265928
the majority of porn/internet addicted retards don't even know this is possible. You have to be in tune together for this to happen and that's not possible if you're of a low vibration

62269694
>>62268709
It's pretty easy if you let her use a wand or clit sucking vibrator to give herself orgasms. The only tricky part is YOU cumming during one of her 5 or 8 orgasms.

62269751
>>62259935
>You're not supposed to check out of society in your 30s and spend the rest of your life relaxing
Why not? Are you a woman? It's usually only parasites annoyed by this.

62269890
>incel bait (((mysteriously))) ruins another anti waging thread
this reddit poster is obviously some low test gooner soiboi. In reality 1.9m in an index fund can get you around 150k a year which is more than enough for a non-incel life especially with a paid off house.

62269901
>>62259935
I'm 37 and once I hit a 2m net worth in the next couple of years I'm going to "retire" and go back into academia. It doesn't pay very well but at least the work is enjoyable and I get to spend time with other scientists instead of these bullshit artists in industry.

62269979
>>62259935
No women? The entire point of FIRE is to fuck wives while the husband works...did you even read the manifesto?

62270348
>>62259935
I'm 40 with only ~$400k net worth. No kids/wife/never been on a date. $25k annual spend.
Just get me a rope...

62270366
How dare you??
You are just a damn child.

62270374
>>62270348
I had $400k net worth when I was 40. I'm 44 now with $1.7 million net worth. A lot can change in a short amount of time.

62270394
>>62270374
>A lot can change in a short amount of time.
Bitcoin's growth is slower now and that's my only hope.
My salary is currently $100k and I don't see potential for any significant raises in my future.
At least I think I'll have the balls to exit the system at $1M and maybe find a part time job stocking shelves to keep me busy.

62270406
>>62270348
You're not missing anything. Society is fucked. I didn't start dating until I was ~33. One woman that I dated when I was 34 ruined me forever. Not financially - just mentally.

I'm worse off financially than you because of student loans but in a few more years, I'll be in better shape albeit still with like half of your net worth when I hit your age. You're in okay shape. You're doing much better than most of your peers. You can get the validation from a woman if you push for it but it won't help. I got validation from other women after the crazy one and I didn't feel a thing. Hopefully you have nieces and nephews for whom you can leave something behind. That's my plan even though I'll probably never even meet my nieces or nephews. So it goes.

62270414
>>62270406
Thanks, bro. I've got my music, I guess.
>nieces and nephews
Nah. I'm a single child raised by a single mother. Once she's gone, I'm all alone.

62270422
>>62270414
Sorry man. My nieces and nephews are the children of my cousins, not any siblings, and I don't have a relationship with my cousins. For all intents and purposes, I'll be pretty alone when my parents are gone and they're both up there in age.

Music is my thing too. I don't enjoy it as much anymore though. Nothing hits the same after that woman. You still have a lot of time to find someone especially since you have your shit together financially but just be careful. I don't want anyone to ever have to feel the way I do on account of a woman.

62270444
>>62270422
I hope things will work out for us in the end.
I'd definitely rather be in a band (something like Jaga Jazzist) than a relationship :) We'll see were things go.

62270945
>>62262891

OnlyFans is always hiring: no experience necessary, work from home at your own hours, no dress code required

62271089
>>62259968
so dont marry. i literally do not understand why you would if you have money and a house?
horny? hire a prostitute.
lonely? get friends, or see above.
existential crisis? realize its fake and gay, we all go into the ground eventually. dead and rotting in the ground.

62271100
>>62270366
i thought the chinese have a huge surplus of men
my mom got pregnant when she was 40 and had me. i only have a touch of 'tism

62271108
>>62268693
All the FIREs are also all bogles. They do not have the brains or cojones to try to get a +3%/year outperformance on the SPY (which I get easily in one of my bad but green months). They do not trust themselves that grand feat.

62271141
>>62263178
Does your "t" button not work?

62271142
>>62261104
Tom Brady is a very swell guy. Everyone should check out his book "The TB12 Method"

62271171
>>62269901
I'm 27, any tips and tricks?

62271254
>>62260803
cant I just do this with crypto and give my mommey the sneed phrase?

62274663
Never retire, goy.

item.title: How do I make money from being a NEET? — 23 replies — 92.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
How do I make money from being a NEET?

Open thread • Replies: 23 • Updated: 2026-05-26 14:31 UTC

OP

How do I make money from being a NEET?

Early replies (first 3)

62272075
Impossible as a man.

62272085
>>62272075
I literally just showed you an example in the OP pic

62272091
>>62272067
I looked into posting AI influencer content on Fanvue because someone on here said it was an easy way to make good money with no skill involved. But you need to upload your ID to Fanvue, there is a fee to use it, and I probably won't make shit on it. I think most of the people shilling making AI influencer content on reddit make money shilling it instead of actually making and posting AI content. In short, it's probably a scam.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62272106
>>62272067
Wildlifecam looks cool.

62272108
>>62272067
This whore isnt even attractive, kek. Who are the losers paying her? Dalit Jeets?

62272110
>>62272091
Online whoring and its adjacents are the modern MLM Home Interiors type of stuff for the once-housewives-now-cat-moms. Very profitable if you are the pimp, good for a select few with the "talent" to make it, and ultimately a waste of time for everyone else involved.

62272206
>>62272067
no she doesn't

62272430
>>62272075
that is a fucking man with an asian pimp husbant.
Just pay 50k for feminization surgeries and you can make 12k per sleep stream once you astroturf yourself for 6 years and build a time machine.

62272438
I had an idea a while ago but never actioned it.
>Setup a local AI, I have A1111
>Have it generate a fictitious woman
>Train it to create the same woman repeatedly
>Keep training it until it can create the exact same woman in any setting/clothing indistinguishable from the other pictures
>Set up an only fans in Hindi
You can see where this is going...

62272442
>>62272438
No where because almost all things you see online are lies.

62272445
>>62272442
They may be lies but that doesn't make them unprofitable, otherwise people wouldn't lie

62272448
>>62272445
bro my president think hes Christ
>people wouldn't lie.
What world do you live in?

62272479
>>62272448
BECAUSE IT'S PROFITABLE
are you retarded?

62272490
>>62272438
>>Set up an only fans in Hindi
It wont even cover your electricity bill

62272500
>>62272490
>Pictures of a European girl in India
I think you underestimate the thirst of half a billion shit machines

62272522
>>62272500
takes one to know one

62272538
>>62272067
that headline is fake by the way
she takes money from a jewish "think-tank" through OF
nobody actually pays anything to her, it's all demoralization theater

62272541
>>62272067
everybody is always asking for how to make money online with no skill but what if i already have skills (programming, sysadmin, video editing, um thats about it) and am willing to learn new stuff

62272552
>>62272538
This

The amount of idiots that take this shit at face value is astounding

62273042
>>62272067
try pooping on camera for money

62273045
>>62272552
Incels believe anything that makes their impotent rage justified they are retarded

62273046
>>62272075
Have high networth

62275087
>>62272500
She knew beforehand. She's enjoying the attention more than Christmas as a kid. She gets off on letting "lesser men" fantasize or fawn over her. She is mistaken in thinking they won't rape her brutally when no one is watching.

item.title: Bitcoin Rainbow Chart Says BUY — 24 replies — 62.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Bitcoin Rainbow Chart Says BUY

Open thread • Replies: 24 • Updated: 2026-05-26 14:08 UTC

OP

This is a once in a lifetime opportunity to buy BTC, STRC, MSTR, ASST, SATA, etc. and earn life changing money.

These next 1-2 years will be huge for Bitcoin.
Everything is about to launch like a rocket ship.
200 day moving average is about to cross at low 80k, all the BTC is being bought up supply squeeze, CLARITY act, banks, institutions, countries, it's the golden bull.

Early replies (first 3)

62270355
>>62270347
So fxcking bullish btc

62270386
>>62270355
Not even joking how do they stop this?

The Rainbow.

62270447
>>62270386
By not buying. Nobody stops you from being a sucker in a ponzi market with no demand

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62270493
>>62270347
SATA and STRC don't scale with Bitcoin though. They're just for free dividend income.

62271070
>>62270347
7k shares of ASST here. $15 avg. Gonna be a sweet sweet ride my friend.

62271073
>>62270493
yup, if u want exponential growth, u ditch sata or strc. They are just financial vehicles to add fuel to the fire.

62271084
The rainbow is DYING

62271223
>>62270493
STRC and SATA are tax deferred dividends.

You earn like 11-13% interest in dividends and instead of paying tax your cost basis just keeps going down, and you pay long term cap gains at the end.
So buy at $100 and that number goes to 99, 98, 95, etc. cost basis as you collect sweet tax free dividends.

MSTR and ASST will probably run hard, but ultimately idk what they'll do with diluting shares and smash buying the top again, lol.

62271438
>>62270347

62271473
>>62270347
What's your price predictions OP

62271761
>>62271473
2026 - 150k
2027 - 500k+

You will see the breakout when we pass the 200 day moving average around 80k to 85k
We are in the bear that you only get every few years (2014, 2018, 2022, 2026)

When the bull is confirmed it'll run into next year and be several hundred k easily.

62271772
>>62271438
It'll go up when it crosses the 200 day moving average.

62271783
Long term holder and closet maxi here. Bitcoin isn't an exponential asset. It's following a power law. That means it takes twice as long to double every doubling. It's going up slower and more sustainably. Of course there can be euphoric rallies but if that happens, don't be the exit liquidity. It will mean revert to a power law.

62271826
>>62271783
The hashrate has been leveling out this bear market, it'll go up soon.
They lost a lot of investment to A.I. companies in the last year.

I think liquidity will dry up first from all the different pools dumping billions into BTC, then the price will run, then we will probably get local top ~150k before the 2nm ASICs get deployed this fall and into 2027.
At that point the real bull market will begin. It'll probably be late 2027 when banks start accepting BTC after the clarity act is ironed out and wallets are developed.

That's why this is the golden bull.
There won't be many more pools of money to dip into. Wallet development will be in overdrive. We're within the last 10 years of ASIC development, mining will get spread out and take over energy projects.

This is it BTC won enjoy the next couple years once we pass that 200 day

62271836
Looks like the rainbow is arching down.

62271875
>>62271826
Quality analysis here.

62271895
>>62270347
So gay.

62271907
>>62271826
Opinion on altcoins?

62271910
>>62271907
Casino.

62271924
>>62271826
Thank you for this hopium. I needed this.

62272136
>>62271783
Actually it follows a double sigmoid. It went up exponentially, then flattened out to give me time to learn about it and buy in. Soon it will go exponential again so that I personally make it.

62272197
>>62272136
This is actually possible. I've always thought comparing bitcoin to mycelium was a useful exercise. The way it grows and lays dormant then grows again.

62274948
>>62272197
Just buy call options of ibit, juice up the strike price to ~100k-150k and wait 2 years.

Thank me later.

item.title: SpaceX has a total addressable market almost the size of the US GDP. — 15 replies — 60.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
SpaceX has a total addressable market almost the size of the US GDP.

Open thread • Replies: 15 • Updated: 2026-05-26 14:28 UTC

OP

SpaceX has a total addressable market almost the size of the US GDP. Why aren't you investing in their IPO?

Early replies (first 3)

62273501
>>62273487
If AI ends up being a nothing burger, then SpaceX is little more than a flashy telecommunications company.
Everyone in the world is betting the farm on AI too, what happens if a different company achieves AGI before SpaceX?

62273502
People still don't understand what printing money does to an economy.

62273506
>>62273487
>SpaceX
>Space
the name of the fucking company is 0.1% of its TAM golly gee maybe they should call it AIX?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62273511
>>62273487
Space is bigger than America so the profits are infinite
Elon really knows how to win

62273537
I'll probably give em a hundred bucks, see how it goes.

62273604
Everyone holding a broad index fund, which is pretty much every American with a retirement account, will be inadvertently investing in this IPO. We will all be Elong Muk's exit liquidity. I can't wait to support a company that loses $5 billion/year.

62273611
>>62273604
Can't you just sell your index fund and then buy it later once the stock has dropped 90%?

62273615
IDK about space, but I'm bullish on tesla and U.S. stocks. I still think rockets are a scam and they already have UFO's that are capable of long distance space travel.

By 2030 solid state battery electric cars will be here in full force in tesla for consumers, making ev's and they'll be exceptional deals, 300K miles with barely any wear compared to a gas engine car. Incredibly efficient compared to gas cars and last longer.

Agentic AI will be in cars, self driving will be good by then.

62273662
>>62273487
"SpaceX estimates it's own TAM by making up a bunch of bullshit"

62273749
>>62273511
Its not, according to muricans.

62274456
>>62273749
Well, it’s not any bigger than Texas.
That’s why they have to launch the rockets from Texas.

62274498
>>62273487
SpaceX TAM eternally va US GDP In one year.

62274836
>>62274498
TAM is measured in annual revenue

62275036
>>62273487
Maybe if he was mining asteroids for precious metals, but this is ridiculous
Twitter is not worth that much money
Grok isn't worth that much money
This is a scam

62275067
>>62275036
Asteroid mining is the kind of thing that sounds like a good idea, until you think about the practicalities of running mining equipment and refining plants in microgravity, the delta-v requirements of bringing the stuff home, and the likely lack of any easily accessible ices that could be turned into rocket propellant on any asteroid that orbits anywhere near the sun

item.title: What do fuddies not understand about $1 per LINK per day? — 14 replies — 56.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
What do fuddies not understand about $1 per LINK per day?

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-05-26 14:24 UTC

OP

What do fuddies not understand about $1 per LINK per day?

Early replies (first 3)

62274691
>>62274690
Hedera CLPR just ate Chainlink's luch.

62274693
flannel shirt is bussy cringe

62274797
>>62274691
>zero iq take
Theyre on the Hedera council dumbass, which means theyre probably the ones going to implement it.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62274806
>>62274797
>Theyre on the Hedera council dumbass
Irrelevant. CLPR makes CCIP obsolete.

62274815
>>62274806
they knew about it before you retard. if hbar really wanted to make enemies they wouldnt have begged chainlink to join their gay little council

62274841
>>62274815
>they knew about it before you retard
Wishful thinking honestly
>f hbar really wanted to make enemies they wouldnt have begged chainlink to join their gay little council
They are out and about explicitly going after bridges with their bridgeless solution

62274848
>>62274690
per day... of the week?
$7?

62274853
>>62274690
Now that ozempic exists he has no excuse to look like an elephant

62274963
>>62274848
$1 per day per link staked = each linkie gets you $365 per year. 1000 staked linkies nets you $365,000.

62274979
>>62274841
>”bridgless solution”
do you know how fucking retarded you sound? if anything hbag will kill xrp, Link is bigger than the entire cryptosphere

62274986
>>62274979
>do you know how fucking retarded you sound?
No I don't. Explain why I'm retarded with actual arguments. Or cope-

62274998
>>62274963
This will never happen without a big Mac being $75.

62275004
>>62274998
This lol

62275045
>>62274690
>Ass unlocked.
>Markets activated.

it's happening bros

item.title: /Biz revival — 116 replies — 39.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
/Biz revival

Open thread • Replies: 116 • Updated: 2026-05-26 11:35 UTC

OP

while this board is clearly dead and all we have left is 10 anons, one of which is a aid devastated bald whiny fag, i think this is the perfect time to try to revive this board.

"How"? i'm hearing you ask.
Well. Let's make this a general where we only share our best picks. NO pointless SHILLING, either stocks or the occasional good crypto you really believe in.


I'll start.

Litecoin is now around 50$. This is a generational bottom if you look on the multi year chart. A solid support. At this price, unless you're convinced altcoins and cryptos are over, it's a free lottery ticket. In the same spirit, LITS, a company which treasury is only composed of its litecoin holding is also a great way to get litecoin exposure.

Ton, the crypto of one if not the most used chatting app worldwide is going back to a historical bottom as we speak after doing a small pump following several changes and pavel durov's statement. (20% staking apy, many other changes planned i won't go into details but good narrative)

A risky stock play but with very good risk / reward ratio: UBISOFT, company went to shit, but they are restructuring and trying to turn things around. A potential 10 bagger over the next years if they can achieve that, and they definitively can if they stop their bullshit.

Early replies (first 3)

62265475
>>62265467
Just buy OpenAI at IPO.
All normalfags will do that and outperform your "smart" plays.
There's such a thing as being too smart.

62265476
>>62265474
yeah that's precisely what we're trying to avoid you fucking retard. Stay poor

62265480
but what if I am bald?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62267354
>>62265467
Lillybux is the next /biz/coin

62267369
>>62265467
greetings anon i have just returned from a hiatus therefore the board is alive again. the only coin you need now is $PUMP. the irony is $PUMP killed crypto by making it possible to launch so many coins that there became no community like there used to be, so now we have to invert that into wealth by buying PUMP. you'll see. bull run hasn't even begun.

62267402
Every single factor that led to GME and CAR going parabolic is perfectly aligned for GRPN in 6 days.

62267687
>>62265467
I regret selling my litecoin and I've bought some of it back. It has a strong narrative as OG coin. There's the livVM being worked on which will bring eth style smart contracts to it. IDK how decentralized it will be but it's a start.

ICP. Do yourself a favor and actually read some of the tech details of how it works. They're setting up "sovereign networks" signing deals with heads of state. Prepping for the future. Chainkey cryptography is wizardry.
The biggest downside, IMO, is the UI is pretty shit - you have to sign a lot of "canisters" (smart contracts) to get going. But it's the only tech that combines blockchain with website plus is reasonably distributed.

62269707
>>62267687
litecoin has developped MWEB network, which is basically private transactions integrated into the already ltc blockchain. it's a pretty big deal, like an integrated coin mixer that is fully private (can't decipher it just like monero).
Very few apps use it yet.
Once it finally gets some sufficient support on any ltc wallet, it will be much like zcash. You go into MWEB, and every public trace stop there. you can also go out of mweb of course.

62269750
Is everyone here stuck in 2017? How about a coin that has come out in the past half decade gramps.

62270867
Ntdoy and rare earth etfs. Some uranium too (nuclear nrgy not nukes)

62270942
I find it so hard to browse this board these days, the humor is so so tiring. Just the same jokes over and over again. I know some people say they find gems here every know and then but I never have, anyone got any advice? I’m so tired man

62271126
>>62265467
Why buy LITS instead of LTC?

62271298
>>62271126
because it's like buying litecoin with a discount, and when ltc pumps LITS will pump multiple X more just like micro strategy did with btc

62271371
>>62265467
>Ton
Turns out that's in my bookmarks as something with potential. I looked at Litecoin when I first got into crypto, but I figured these long-standing projects like it, LINK, HBAR, etc. were things I'd come back to later with gains from lower market cap stuff. Seems that was the correct decision since so many of them are now around the same price as they day I bought my first assets.
I figured they might make great strides someday but not in a hurry since they'd already make great strides (price-wise) since their debuts. Despite that I'm shocked to see Litecoin is like half the price of when I first looked at it.
I'd recommend some stuff myself but I'm still in that newish area where I've made good picks after reading up on stuff before buying (the DYOR part /biz/ always told me to do) but couldn't briefly articulate why someone should take a look at something at current prices. Everything that's done me well is more expensive now than when I first bought it.

62271377
>>62265877
I don't think there's a LUNC thread up now but I remember seeing one recently where there was a lot of reasonable optimism.

62271378
>>62265467
It's dead Jim

62271387
>>62270867
What's the basis for uranium at this time? I saw a few months ago people talking about various reactors, from new mini ones to restarting some existing ones.

62271407
>>62271387
an anon shilled some us stock, nano nuclear energy and i didn't buy because the chart looked already pumped. it went down a lot since like 40%

Nano nuclear energy. I looked it up they're trying to develop mini nuclear reactors thant can be moved around, like for data centers etc. But i'm wondering isn't that tech super risky?in any case that might or might not be a great moonshot. No idea, don't own any but looking at it

62271421
>>62271407
I can see what you would be. I remember seeing some airplane-lifted reactors some months ago. Operation Windlord. Valar Atomics, though, they are not publicly traded.
I see what you mean about NNE having doing about a 10x since it began and now being like 50% down since then. Still, nice to have specific leads on what to research.

62271501
>>62265467
Its dead because there havent been enough nigger days due to the bull market

62271653
How about starting with removing this faggot nigger captcha and having to wait for a minute for it? I gave up posting here, because of it, honestly. And I'm not verifying my email each time either.

62271675
>>62271653
Captchas become faster and easier if you post more

62271760
>>62271675
and then reset your progress for no reason
no, I didn't erase my cookies, just couldn't post for TWO DAYS

62271767
>>62271760
yes jannies trannies destroyed it all. it's over. At least biz is the only white board left

62271790
>>62265467
good pick with litecoin anon, literally the only coin which can reliably process frequent micropayments without clogging or taking too many fees


it will be the coin of choice for autonomous AI agents to transact with eachother in the future

62271899
>>62265467
what killed this place to begin with is that there is no business or finance talk going on here. Its just retarded "buy this crypto coin!!!"

62272124
>>62265467
>LTC
Abandoned project. $50 stable ass coin.

>TON
Shit is never going to pump.

>UBISOFT, company went to shit, but they are restructuring and trying to turn things around. A potential 10 bagger over the next years if they can achieve that, and they definitively can if they stop their bullshit.
>IF
They won't ever back down their bullshit, even in the deepest shit they still prefered to appeal to niggers and fags instead of making games.

62272342
>>62272124
>Abandoned project. $50 stable ass coin.
they just developped MWEB transactions anon. Try harder.

why wouldn't ton pump? are you retarded?

nvgmi

62272366
>>62265467
india and china need to be banned from this board

62273281
>>62271298
Seems like it crashes X times more too unfortunately. Maybe a good idea for ltc exposure but I’m really unsure if litecoin will be the play next bull

62273290
>>62265467
>we need to revive biz
>how do we do it you ask
>well its simple you just buy my decade old bags of a shitcoin so i can finally break even
well guess we found aid devastated bald whiny fag and its op

62274410
>>62269707
Yeah I think Litecoin "trojan horses" privacy into mainstream cryptocurrency. The marketing around it has always been "OG coin".
Eventually the markets will realize privacy is useful for more than cipherpunkery, and is embraced by big business.
Related, there was a lot of ZK research done by Polygon teams, but their funding ran out, I think? There's some other smaller projects experimenting with very compact proofs, Mina Protocol I think. I haven't researched that much. I'm a fan of LTC for being OG - strong codebase.

62274471
>>62273281
crashes x times more

not really. right now the price of the stock is like a 20% discount over their litecoin treasury. it gets giga hyped if litecoin moons. Now if litecoin were to moon for real one day (let's say 800$) this stock would probably x 50 from where it is.
The thing i don't like about LITS is basically not your key, not your cryptos. So i'm 30% LITS 70% litecoin

>>62274410
it's really a revolution. I think btc will implement the same thing. It's an integrated coin mixer that can't be traced basically.

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-05-26 14:20 UTC

OP

Remember when Qubic 51% attacked Monero with a handful of retards but anons on here ignored it and doubled down that no governments would EVER be able to do anything to their heckin secret squirrel coin

Early replies (first 3)

62273717
>>62273459
Nobody 51%d mobero.

62273801
>>62273459
>when Qubic 51% attacked
False

62273913
>>62273459
>optional
>privacy
kek
lol
lmfao

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62273928
>>62273913
XD silly zion jews

62274700
>>62273459
Hey, can you show us the stats when they had 51%? What, they got 40% at peak and despite calling for more attacks they never managed to get that high ever again? Try again, shill.

62275020
>>62274700
>using Principia Mathematica to prove 1+1 = 2
CFB is such a midwit for this. Here. Let me show you how easy it is to prove 1+1 = 2 in the natural (read whole positive and zero) numbers using the more modern Peano axioms. Explanation added so that anyone could understand it (hopefully)
>Definition: 0 is a natural number
>Definition: let S(n) denote the successor to a natural number n s.t. S(n) = S(m) <=> n = m. Let the natural numbers be closed under S. (i.e. all natural numbers can be generated from adding 1 to 0 over and over again)
>Remark: 1 stands for S(0) and 2 for S(S(0)). (read: 1 is 0+1 and 2 is 0+1+1, this isn't the addition operator here though)
>Definition: addition of n + m:
>n 0 = n
>n S(m) = S(n + m)
>Remark: Here we are saying that the sum of any two numbers can be constructed by taking one away from the last and adding it to the first. Because all natural numbers are just 0+1*something, we are bound to reach zero. In which case we stop the process of addition and return.
>Proof: 1 + 1 = 2 by applying the definition of addition:
>1 + 1 = S(1 + 0) = S(1) = 2
QED (we're done, nothing more to show)
There. 4 statements. Principia Matematica is part of the history of mathematics, but nobody really uses its construction for the natural numbers anymore. It does appear as a piece of trivia about how hard basic mathematics really is. Which it isn't. It just takes time and effort. CFB is being incredibly obtuse on purpose here and I highly doubt he knows what half of those symbols mean in the screenshot.
>t. math major in university

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Dm_BrGu1sHM

You can chase your fantasy. It's not a mirage.

62269716

62269721

62269752
They should make good games again, not some nigger samurai goyslop.

62269757
if gta flops (which it will) this will follow, then the rest of the gaming industry will die, video games are dying

62269758
somehow this piece of shit still feels overvalued

62269782
>>62268847
Name three companies that pulled off a resurgence ever since becoming woke AND poorly made. Call of duty is resurging. Halo isn't resurging. Fallout isn't resurging.

62269827
massive /v/ fag here who follows the industry.

Heres the thing with this.

The owners/family of Ubisoft are flat out fucking retarded and French. Internally they are oversaturated with toxic positivity and literal faux progressiveness but BELIEVE IT fully(this is important) its past DEI.

Yes they have IPs but dev cycles are long, 4-6-8 years. Games like theirs cost shitloads of money(they dont actually but they spend it) and generally run tandem Dev teams to hit cycles with proper pacing for the audience. Example(this is just fake)

Ass Creed starts pre-production----> 1 year ----> Far Cry starts Pre-Production----> 2 years ----> Ass creed in production ---->3 years ----> Far Cry production ----2 years but its total 3 still.

Ass Creed Marketing into final release-----> 4th year-----> release ----Far Cry in 3rd Year-----> 5th year Far Cry releases 4 years with marketing leading.

You have to take in consideration WHO is leading them, whos on the teams, and how retarded they are being, Ubisoft has a fantastic track record of being retarded AND not adjusting the course.

Nintendo and Capcom DO act retarded, then adjust course, especially Capcom, basically their MO. Ubisoft has been on a chronic implosion with no end in sight.

Investing in this is a gamble, it can pay off, but this is the final shot. Might as well since stocks are so cheap but dont expect it to go anywhere with a gureentee. They are backed by French Tax money from the government as well. They were called out for being retarded in one of the last shareholder meetings specifically about DEI shit. They doubled down.

If they sell of IPs Ubisoft doesnt gain value, they lose it. The fact they are on a trove of IPs is solid, but if they keep fucking it up, their image will just finally go past the point of no return. They have a dogshit and invasive PC platform forced on you to play their shit games EVEN if bought through steam.

The games are worse, support is worse, overall its bad.

62269947
>>62269827
Frenchfag here, the owners of Ubisoft are from Brittany, which is the HQ of woke leftist dreamers completely disconnected from reality. Employees (who mostly look like leftards when you see them protesting) also say that the CEO is senile like Trump. So yeah, not a good look.

>>62269255
UBI spends so much it could also go under and be bought by Tencent for €1 in the end. That’s the risk.

62269958
>>62268842
Yes

62270256
>>62269782
EA sold 40billion or something. It was the most hated company by gamers for years back in 2012 and 2013.

Now name a gaming company that has went bankrupt with multiple IP's that are valued for billions, have their own game engine. If Embracer can't get bankrupt, I doubt Ubi, France's darling and Tencent's investment, will go bankrupt.

62270276
>>62269947
>UBI spends so much it could also go under
Firing can recoup the spending. They have assets worth more than the market cap atm. Tencent already gave them 1billion to live. The issue is, the board has a deal with the Guillemot family to turnaround the company till 2030 to not do an agressive takeover.

62270298
>>62270256
>with multiple IP's that are valued for billion
They co-own with Tencent.

62270478
>>62270298
They have 26% ownership of 3 IP's with zero control with an agreement where Ubi can buy it back. It's essential a IP backed loan.

62270729
>>62268804

62270743

They have debt on top of that and had a terrible fiscal year.

62271812

62271815
>>62271812

62271837
>>62269947

You would think being about to bankrupt a billion $ business can make people rethink their strategy? at this point if they have ot make a game where you're a white supremacist hunting down brown people they will. Nobody believes anything in business, except profit.

62272462
Because its so fucking low it is probably worth pumping in 1-2 days of wagie wage. Even low jobs can put in a little over $100. Horrible idea to go big.


They have to turn it around in any way by 2030. Ass creed 4 remake cant unless they do the smart thing and make it fantastic, optimized, and like $40.


Basically it needs to be a massive PR success. Anything less and ubis fucked, rayman doesnt have the reach.

The only other thing is a hidden hail mary. This can happen(capcoms RE7 saved the franchise.


For non-/v/fags what needs to happen is a strong PR release, then continued momentum. Capcom succeeded with this doing RE7—>DMC5——>mh world. 3 loved series, all good games regardless of personal opinion. Ubisoft doesnt have that luxury. All 3 of those games had major audience “want” if that makes sense.

62273671

62273679

item.title: What is your profit taking strategy? — 4 replies — 16.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
What is your profit taking strategy?

Open thread • Replies: 4 • Updated: 2026-05-26 14:22 UTC

OP

When to take profits for someone new in the stock market?
This is a position I'd like to keep in the long run. It seems logical to sell and keep that as a reserve for when the stock sinks again. This ticker in particular has exploded way beyond expectation in a month.

>I here once it reaches 100% return, one should take 25-30% of profits.


What would be best here? News is saying this company is in backlog till 2028 and is in demand so long the AI-trend continues. They are saying this will increase +25% EOY, but I am quite nervous on this growing this much.

Early replies (first 3)

62274764
you better get a job if you are excited about 800 dollar gains

62274873
My strategy is to sell everything in one trade. My targets are conservative. If there isn't enough DEX liquidity, I sell OTC.

62275003
>>62274873
I'm not talking about shitcoins. I mean a real company I'm willing to hold for the next 5 years.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62275033
>>62274755
>open position
>wait for it to get 10-15% in green
>close position
>keep 80% of profit, use remaining 20% for making the next position bigger
>repeat

item.title: What’s stopping you from buying BTC? — 76 replies — 56.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
What’s stopping you from buying BTC?

Open thread • Replies: 76 • Updated: 2026-05-26 13:11 UTC

OP

What is it? Fear? Lack of conviction? Waiting for lower prices? Or do you genuinely think Bitcoin fails long term?

Curious what’s holding people back at this stage.

Early replies (first 3)

62271721
>>62271712
Btc needs a catalyst. It probably will be yield curve control with a new name. It could also be codifying the SBR into law which is less likely. Or my personal favorite schizo theory, it's discovered that Iran was mining and selling bitcoin to fund it's endeavors and that their supply is finally exhausted.

62271922
>>62271712
Gays in it.

62271933
>>62271712
everybody and their femboy lovers above two-digits IQ are waiting for BTC to dump below at least 40k by July. Why anybody buy now? 4 years cycles never lie

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62273111
>>62272929
I've been wrong before but mostly get macro outlook from Rick Rule, Dalio (mostly for social political) and Luke Gromen. From these inflation is unavoidable being several times what the government will admit to and the stock market going broadly up is over. Stock picking will overperform everything if intelligent and crypto is at present sabotaged by everything the government is doing, even if they planned to bail themselves out short term using genius act for additional funding. Gromen was a BTC bull but sold relatively near the top at ~93k and got major shit for it but thought BTC is acting odd and crypto being hobbled by the US directly and indirectly is not particularly good looking for the near term. It would be different if BTC was used for international settlement like gold is currently but it has risks of being weaponized and seized. So long as the US wants to be retarded and use assets as weapons commodity producers and buyers will trend towards things that the US cannot manipulate, the underlying citizens do they same for the most part. Stable coins had a chance if the price of fuel and staple grains hadn't skyrocketted for the forseeable future and this likely would have been bullish for BTC.

62273196
>>62271712

62273541
>>62271712
It's the long play. I dca into it and don't give it much attention. I have cold storage. Same as Silver or gold I'll buy when I can regularly and let it stack. There's also allot of pessimism from this board about it so when I talk about it it's somewhere else. Here I'd rather talk about an ETF I'm interested in or some common stock I think can move. BTC isn't going anywhere. Crypto is just very dull during bear markets so I tune out and dca as many others do.

62273547
>>62271712
I have one already and I'd rather gamble on alts now

62273575
>>62271712
I will never buy a single crypto again.
Unfortunately for me, I chose alts over BTC. My mistake, but I'm too jaded at this stage to even consider buying BTC.
It's all garbage to me. I'm out. Sticking to boomer index funds.

62273583
>>62273575
good goy knowing his place

62273635
>>62272623
Why risk your btc in centralised services again and again

62273638
>>62273575
Garbage? I think you don't have money to buy btc at this level. Poor

62273643
>>62273547

62273653
>>62273638
Oh but I do.
I'm just basically not going to buy any.
Sorry, not being your exit liquidity again. Have done that enough over the years.

62273655
no value, no real use. i dont believe in it. i dont care if it goes to 1million

62273660
>>62271712
>What’s stopping you from buying BTC?
I don't like people who own it and sell it. I don't want to give them my real money. I think it would be good if everyone stopped giving them money. They don't deserve the lavish lives that they are living by selling bitcoin to people like you.

62273787
>>62273635

A lot of people still don’t understand the native staking and self-custody side of BTCFi yet.

62273791
>>62271965

62273794
>>62272143

62273812
>>62272874
>moving huge wealth out of a country
Only brown people leave their home country. Only criminals do tax evasion. I'm neither of those.

62273814
>>62271712
lack of desire to throw my money away to a kike scam

62273819
>>62271935

62273824
>>62272659
>too bad you didnt buy at $200 or you'd be retired
retired on what? US dollars from selling your shitty worthless digital ponzi coin? so you admit bitcoin has no purpose or use other than hoping other retards buy it to pump up the price so you can exit before it crashes?

62273959
>>62273824
So you're buying stocks in the hope some retards will pump the price so you can exit before it crashes?

62273985
>>62271712
The narrative keeps shifting. First it was supposed to finally defeat the dollar, then it was supposed to be ATMs everywhere, then it's supposed to be digital gold, then the government and blackrock buy it, then it's not SUPPOSED to be in atms and then it's just really a leverage tech stock without any tech.

It's insanity. How am I supposed to know what the price will be in 10 years if the narrative cycle is like in 2?

62273994
>>62273787
This guy who spams this in every discussion has convinced me that what he's talking about is a scam and I'll never use it.

62274011
>>62271712
I have a little bitcoin, but I need large amounts of cash on hand.

62274038
>>62273994
>DYOR
>he does not DYOR
Doesn't matter that much though since you can just keep scaling by putting gains into new assets rather than ever staking.

62274120
Let's buy more BTC

62274282
>>62273959
yes beause stocks represnt ownership in a company which is a real asset with actual cashflow unlike a digital ponzi based on a generic internet token with zero utility that anyone can make at any time

also im not pretending stocks are the future currency of the world that everyone is going to use like the retard dipshits such as yourself

62274315
>>62273655
>i dont care if it goes to 1million
the /biz/ way

62274703
>>62273994

otherwise GTFO!

62274708
